description:
You are acting as a platform-engineering SRE who has just been asked to attach a lightweight metrics store to an existing CI/CD pipeline. A brand-new, file-based SQLite database is all that is needed for now. What you must deliver -------------------- Inside your home directory create a sub-directory called “cicd_db_work” and prepare **three files** in it exactly as described below. 1. /home/user/cicd_db_work/ci_cd_metrics.db • SQLite database file that contains a single table named **build_metrics** with the precise schema shown under “Table definition” below. • The table must hold the three rows shown under “Seed data”. 2. /home/user/cicd_db_work/build_time_average.txt • A one-line text file that shows the average of the *build_time_seconds* column, rounded to exactly two decimal places. • The required format is (no extra spaces, one trailing newline): average_build_time_seconds:<value_with_two_decimals> Example (do **not** hard-code): average_build_time_seconds:348.33
3. /home/user/cicd_db_work/cicd_sql_audit.log • A plain-text audit log in which every SQL statement you executed while constructing the DB is written, in the exact order executed. • Each statement must occupy exactly one line, end with a single “;”, and have no leading / trailing spaces. • The file must contain *only* those lines—no comments, no blank lines. 4. /home/user/cicd_db_work/metrics_export.csv • A CSV export of the entire **build_metrics** table, including a header row, using commas as separators and **no** surrounding quotes. • Lines must be terminated with Unix newlines (\n). Table definition ---------------- CREATE TABLE build_metrics ( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, pipeline_name TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('success','failed')) NOT NULL, build_time_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL, build_date DATE NOT NULL ); Seed data (exactly these three rows, in this order) --------------------------------------------------- 1 | web-frontend | success | 420 | 2023-09-01 2 | api-service | failed | 370 | 2023-09-01 3 | database-migrations | success | 255 | 2023-09-02 Acceptance checks performed by the grader ----------------------------------------- 1. The directory /home/user/cicd_db_work exists. 2. The SQLite database file exists and can be opened; PRAGMA integrity_check returns “ok”. 3. `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM build_metrics;` returns 3. 4. The data in the table matches the “Seed data” section exactly. 5. The content of build_time_average.txt matches the specified template and the value equals `SELECT ROUND(AVG(build_time_seconds),2) FROM build_metrics;`. 6. metrics_export.csv matches the full table dump, including header row, newlines and order. 7. cicd_sql_audit.log contains one line per SQL statement executed; each line ends with “;”. (The grader checks the presence of the CREATE TABLE statement and the three INSERT statements at a minimum.) Operational constraints ----------------------- • Work strictly inside /home/user and below; no root privileges will be available. • Use only standard Linux CLI tools plus the `sqlite3` shell (already installed in the execution environment). • The whole job can be done in 5–10 shell commands; use whichever sequence you prefer, but make sure the final artefacts match the specifications above. When you are finished, verify your work by running: ls -R /home/user/cicd_db_work sqlite3 /home/user/cicd_db_work/ci_cd_metrics.db '.schema build_metrics' sqlite3 /home/user/cicd_db_work/ci_cd_metrics.db 'SELECT * FROM build_metrics;' cat /home/user/cicd_db_work/build_time_average.txt cat /home/user/cicd_db_work/metrics_export.csv cat /home/user/cicd_db_work/cicd_sql_audit.log Only proceed to the next task once all outputs look correct.
truth:
<!-- Directory that must exist -->
    /home/user/cicd_db_work

    <!-- SQLite DB verification -->
    /home/user/cicd_db_work/ci_cd_metrics.db  (file must exist)

    Expected table schema inside ci_cd_metrics.db:
    CREATE TABLE build_metrics (
        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
        pipeline_name TEXT NOT NULL,
        status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('success','failed')) NOT NULL,
        build_time_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL,
        build_date DATE NOT NULL
    );

    Expected rows (in order returned by SELECT * FROM build_metrics ORDER BY id):
    1|web-frontend|success|420|2023-09-01
    2|api-service|failed|370|2023-09-01
    3|database-migrations|success|255|2023-09-02

    <!-- Derived numeric value -->
    Calculated average build_time_seconds (rounded 2 decimals): 348.33

    <!-- build_time_average.txt -->
    Full path: /home/user/cicd_db_work/build_time_average.txt
    Content exactly (with trailing newline):
    average_build_time_seconds:348.33\n

    <!-- metrics_export.csv -->
    Full path: /home/user/cicd_db_work/metrics_export.csv
    Content exactly (each line terminated with \n):

    id,pipeline_name,status,build_time_seconds,build_date\n
    1,web-frontend,success,420,2023-09-01\n
    2,api-service,failed,370,2023-09-01\n
    3,database-migrations,success,255,2023-09-02\n

    <!-- cicd_sql_audit.log -->
    Full path: /home/user/cicd_db_work/cicd_sql_audit.log
    Expected minimal required lines (each ending with \n):

    CREATE TABLE build_metrics (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, pipeline_name TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('success','failed')) NOT NULL, build_time_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL, build_date DATE NOT NULL);\n
    INSERT INTO build_metrics (id, pipeline_name, status, build_time_seconds, build_date) VALUES (1,'web-frontend','success',420,'2023-09-01');\n
    INSERT INTO build_metrics (id, pipeline_name, status, build_time_seconds, build_date) VALUES (2,'api-service','failed',370,'2023-09-01');\n
    INSERT INTO build_metrics (id, pipeline_name, status, build_time_seconds, build_date) VALUES (3,'database-migrations','success',255,'2023-09-02');\n

    The grader will allow additional valid SQL statements (e.g., the SELECT or .mode directives) as long as each is on its own line, ends with “;”, and the four lines above are present in any order.
